methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Teramount operates in Chips & Semiconductors as an acquired-stage company that has raised $50M+. The valuation landscape comprises 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Standard triangulation methods yield limited utility here. Peer-Median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage peers within Chips & Semiconductors; while 14 same-sector companies exist in the dataset, none satisfy simultaneous filters for stage, timing, and valuation tier—a minimum of 3 datapoints is required for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be deployed because Teramount's revenue is not on file, a common constraint in private company valuations. The public semiconductor universe shows a median P/S multiple of 24.2x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD, but this cannot be applied without the target's top-line figure.

Given the acquisition stage and data constraints, this valuation resists quantitative triangulation. The company's position within a reasonably sized peer set of 11 total comparables—both public and private—provides directional context but no supportable point estimate or range. Any valuation conclusion would require either disclosure of Teramount's revenue, identification of tighter stage-matched private peers with disclosed metrics, or supplementary data on transaction multiples within the semiconductor subsector. The current dataset supports only the acknowledgment that methodological constraints preclude a defensible valuation.
